title: cornus stolonifera subsp. occidentalis
intro: Cornus stolonifera subsp. occidentalis, also known as the western flowering dogwood, is a deciduous shrub native to North America, characterized by its distinctive white or pink flowers and red berries. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can aid in the treatment of wounds, infections, and digestive issues. The health benefits of Cornus stolonifera subsp. occidentalis include its ability to lower blood pressure, reduce inflammation, and improve cardiovascular health. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are used to support overall well-being and promote relaxation.
